label: agentdb • ORDER BY + LIMIT to find the runner-up
query: Your agent database has a table scores(player TEXT, points INTEGER). List the top 2 players by points in descending order, then tell me the name of the runner-up (the second-highest).
notes: Ranking read: requires ORDER BY points DESC + LIMIT, then picking the SECOND row. 'dan' (40) leads, 'ben' (30) is the runner-up. toolUsageAudit pins that the model actually used a limit rather than slurping the table; finalTextContains pins the runner-up name.

seedSql: CREATE TABLE scores (player TEXT, points INTEGER); INSERT INTO scores (player, points) VALUES ('amy',10),('ben',30),('cat',20),('dan',40);

dbState.sql: SELECT player FROM scores ORDER BY points DESC LIMIT 1
